content.body:
Vector: has both magnitude AND direction
Scalar: magnitude only (mass, speed, temperature)

Vector notation: bold a, or a⃗, or AB⃗
Magnitude: |a|

Column vector: a = (x/y) or (x,y)
|a| = √(x²+y²)
Unit vector: magnitude = 1, â = a/|a|
Unit vectors i=(1,0), j=(0,1)

content.title: Vector Operations
content.body:
a=(3,4): |a|=√(9+16)=5
Unit vector: â=(3/5, 4/5)

Addition: (2,3)+(1,-1)=(3,2)
Subtraction: (5,2)-(1,4)=(4,-2)
Scalar multiple: 3(2,1)=(6,3)

Position vector of point P(3,4): OP⃗=(3,4)

content.title: Midpoints and Division
content.body:
Midpoint M of AB: OM⃗=(OA⃗+OB⃗)/2
A(2,3), B(6,7): M=((2+6)/2,(3+7)/2)=(4,5)

Section formula (ratio m:n):
Point dividing AB in m:n=(nOA+mOB)/(m+n)

questions.question: Vector is different from scalar because
options: vector has direction as well as magnitude
questions.answer: vector has direction as well as magnitude
questions.explanation: Scalar: size only. Vector: size AND direction (e.g. velocity vs speed)

questions.question: Unit vector in direction (6,8)
questions.explanation: Magnitude=10. Unit vector=(6/10,8/10)=(0.6,0.8)
Add components: (2+3,5-2)=(5,3)
Scalar multiply each component: (6,-2)

questions.question: Position vector of P(5,-2) from origin
questions.explanation: Position vector = coordinates of point = (5,-2)

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body:
Dot (scalar) product:
a·b = |a||b|cosθ
a·b = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂

If a·b = 0 → vectors perpendicular (θ=90°)
If a·b = |a||b| → parallel (θ=0°)

cosθ = a·b/(|a||b|)

Vector projection of a onto b:
proj = (a·b/|b|²)b

content.title: Work Done
content.body:
Work = F·d = |F||d|cosθ
Force (3,4)N moves object along (2,6)m
W=(3)(2)+(4)(6)=6+24=30 J

questions.question: Vectors perpendicular if dot product is
questions.explanation: a·b=|a||b|cosθ=0 ↔ cosθ=0 ↔ θ=90°

questions.question: cosθ for a=(3,0) and b=(0,3)
questions.explanation: a·b=0, so cosθ=0, θ=90°

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Vectors Intro. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Vectors Intro concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of mathematics.
Core Theory

This section of Vectors Intro builds on foundational knowledge. In mathematics, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 3.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Vectors Intro. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 3.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Vectors Intro. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.
Common examination questions on Vectors Intro include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Vectors Intro. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a like term with 3x²?
questions.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable AND the same power. 3x has power 1, not 2, so it is not a like term with 3x².

questions.question: What is the gradient/slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so gradient = rise/run = 0/run = 0.

questions.question: If a = 4 and b = 3, find 2a + b.
questions.explanation: Substitute: 2(4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What does the inequality x > 5 mean?
questions.explanation: The > symbol means 'greater than'. x > 5 means x takes values greater than (but not equal to) 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) = 14
questions.explanation: Expand: 2x + 6 = 14. Subtract 6: 2x = 8. Divide: x = 4.

questions.question: The HCF of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common factor = 6.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,... Multiples of 6: 6,12,... Lowest common multiple = 12.

questions.question: Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself. 7 is prime (factors: 1 and 7 only).
